page: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, featuring an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated virtual CPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage capabilities.

Performance benchmarks indicate a divergence between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core tests, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core, rank in the average tier. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall into the weak category, reflecting the low physical core count. Memory bandwidth tests show weak read performance for both small and large blocks, though write bandwidth for large blocks remains in the average range. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as PassMark CPU encryption and single-threaded gzip compression, perform at average levels.

The primary tradeoff of the E16-4s_v5 is its high memory capacity paired with limited compute resources. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require large memory footprints but do not demand intensive multi-threaded CPU processing. It is suitable for memory-intensive applications such as caching layers, small to medium databases with low concurrency, and lightweight data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores, 2 threads per core) clocked at 2.8 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on remote Premium Storage. Benchmark data reveals average single-core CPU performance and average memory write bandwidth, contrasted with weak multi-core CPU and memory read performance. This server is structured for memory-heavy workloads with moderate compute requirements, such as database operations, caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute needs.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16-4s_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, running on Hyper-V.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute requirements.

128.0 GB of RAM providing a high 32.0 GB per core memory density
